title: 10 best herbal decoctions for fever
intro_desc: Herbal decoctions for fever are a traditional remedy used to alleviate symptoms associated with fevers, such as body aches, headaches, and sweating. These natural remedies work by increasing sweat production, which helps to expel toxins and reduce fever temperatures. For example, ginger and cinnamon decoctions have been shown to effectively lower fevers in children, while elderberry and peppermint decoctions can help soothe symptoms of colds and flu. By using herbal decoctions for fever, individuals can experience relief from discomfort and improve overall quality of life.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger decoctions helps with fever because of its natural ability to reduce inflammation and body heat. The active compounds in ginger, such as gingerols and shogaols, have a potent anti-inflammatory effect that can help bring down elevated body temperatures. Additionally, the diaphoretic properties of ginger stimulate sweating, allowing the body to rid itself of excess heat and toxins more efficiently. This natural remedy has been used for centuries to alleviate fever symptoms, providing relief from discomfort and promoting overall wellness.
remedy_properties: Antipyretic properties: Ginger decoction helps to reduce body temperature by increasing sweat production and promoting blood circulation, which can alleviate symptoms of fever.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Ginger's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as gingerol and shogaol, help to reduce inflammation in the body, which can exacerbate fever and other symptoms associated with infection or illness.
Digestive stimulant properties: Ginger decoction can aid digestion and relieve nausea and vomiting, common symptoms of fever, by stimulating the digestive system and reducing stomach contractions.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in natural oils and compounds like gingerol, which helps to reduce body temperature and alleviate fever symptoms.
Rhyzomes: Contains active compounds like shogaol, which has anti-inflammatory properties and can help to reduce fever-related inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh ginger roots of at least 2 cm in length for optimal potency.
Wash the ginger roots thoroughly under cold running water to remove any dirt or debris.
Grate the washed ginger roots into small pieces using a grater or food processor.
Combine one teaspoon of grated ginger with one cup of boiling water and let it simmer for 5 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a clean container and discard solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kansas coneflower decoctions helps with fever because of its unique combination of bioactive compounds, including triterpenoids and polyphenols. These compounds have been shown to possess potent anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties, which can help reduce the severity of febrile symptoms by alleviating inflammation and viral replication. By incorporating these natural compounds into a decoction, individuals may experience a rapid and effective reduction in fever, making Kansas coneflower an increasingly popular herbal remedy for soothing feverish discomforts.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Kansas Coneflower contains anti-inflammatory compounds like caffeic acid and chicoric acid, which help reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with fever.
Antimicrobial activity: The decoction's antimicrobial properties can help combat the underlying infection or bacteria causing the fever, thereby reducing its severity and duration.

Immunomodulatory effects: Echinacea angustifolia has been shown to stimulate the immune system, enhancing the body's natural defenses against infection and reducing the risk of complications related to fever.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in active compounds that help to reduce body temperature and alleviate symptoms associated with fever.
Seeds: Seeds contain oils that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe and calm the body during a fever.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 grams of dried echinacea angustifolia roots and stems from a trusted supplier or local apothecary.
Rinse the herbs in cool running water to remove any impurities or debris.
Combine the rinsed herbs with 500 milliliters of distilled water in a medium-sized pot and bring to a boil.
Reduce heat to low and simmer for 20-30 minutes, allowing the herbs to release their medicinal properties into the liquid.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass container, discarding solids and preserving the resulting liquid for future use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow decoctions helps with fever because of its unique combination of bioactive compounds, including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenes. These compounds work together to reduce inflammation, alleviate discomfort, and promote sweating, all of which can help to bring down a fever. Additionally, yarrow's natural antimicrobial properties may help to combat the underlying infection or illness causing the fever. As the decoction is consumed, its active ingredients are absorbed into the body, providing relief from fever-related symptoms and promoting recovery.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Yarrow contains sesquiterpenes and flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory effects, helping to reduce fever-related inflammation and discomfort.
Antipyretic properties: Yarrow's antipyretic compounds, such as apigenin and luteolin, help lower body temperature by increasing sweating, making it an effective natural remedy for reducing fever.
Antioxidant activity: Yarrow's antioxidant properties help protect the body from oxidative stress caused by fever, which can damage cells and tissues, thus promoting overall recovery.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used due to their high concentration of alkaloids and flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antipyretic properties, making them effective in reducing fever.
Leaves: Yarrow leaves contain a high amount of apigenin, a flavonoid that has been shown to have antiviral and antibacterial properties, helping to combat the underlying causes of fever.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried achillea millefolium leaves and flowers in quantities of 20-30 grams.
Rinse the harvested material with cold water to remove any dirt or debris.
Combine the rinsed plant material with 500-700 milliliters of boiling water in a heat-resistant glass container.
Steep the mixture for 10-15 minutes, then strain and discard the solids using a coffee filter or cheesecloth.
Store the decoction in an amber glass bottle at room temperature for up to 2 days before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint decoctions helps with fever because of its natural cooling properties, which can help to reduce body temperature. The menthol content in peppermint has a calming effect on the skin, helping to soothe headaches and relieve congestion caused by fever. Additionally, the anti-inflammatory properties of peppermint can help to alleviate pain and discomfort associated with fever. By drinking peppermint decoctions, individuals can experience relief from fever-related symptoms and promote overall well-being.
remedy_properties: Menthol: Menthol, a key component of peppermint oil, helps to cool the body down by stimulating sweat production, which can help to reduce body temperature and alleviate symptoms of fever.

Eugenol: Eugenol, another active compound found in peppermint, exhibits anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, making it effective in reducing inflammation and relieving headache and body aches associated with fever.

Volatile oils: The volatile oils present in peppermint decoctions have antiseptic and antibacterial properties, which help to combat infection-causing pathogens and promote healing in the body, thereby helping to alleviate symptoms of fever.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in menthol and other volatile oils, peppermint roots have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making them effective in reducing fever and relieving body aches.

Rhyzomes: The rhizome of peppermint contains sesquiterpenes, which help to reduce inflammation, relax muscles, and soothe the digestive system, making it an excellent part for treating fever-related discomforts.
remedy_recipe: Gather fresh or dried mentha x piperita leaves and stems for a total of two tablespoons.
Measure one quart of water and bring it to a boil in a large pot over high heat immediately.
Add the prepared mentha x piperita herbs to the boiling water and reduce heat to medium simmer slowly.
Allow the mixture to steep for 15 minutes before straining with a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve carefully.
Transfer the strained decoction to a clean glass bottle and store it in the refrigerator for up to three days.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice decoctions helps with fever because it has anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties that help reduce the body's temperature. The licorice root contains compounds like glycyrrhizin, which has been shown to inhibit the replication of viruses and bacteria that cause fever. When consumed as a decoction, these compounds are absorbed into the bloodstream and work to calm the body's inflammatory response, helping to bring down a high fever quickly and safely. Additionally, licorice decoctions have a soothing effect on the throat and chest, providing relief from congestion and coughing associated with fever.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Licorice root contains anti-inflammatory compounds like glycyrrhizin, which can help reduce inflammation and swelling associated with fever, making it more manageable.

Antiviral and antibacterial properties: Licorice root has been shown to possess antiviral and antibacterial properties, which can help combat the underlying cause of the fever, such as a viral or bacterial infection.

Expectorant and mucolytic effects: Licorice root's expectorant and mucolytic properties can help loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, relieving congestion and cough associated with fever, making it easier to breathe.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of licorice contain glycyrrhizin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties, making it effective in reducing fever.
Rhyzomes: Licorice rhyzomes have been used to treat various fevers, including malaria, due to their ability to reduce inflammation and kill pathogens.
remedy_recipe: Measure out 2 teaspoons of dried glycyrrhiza glabra root powder.
Boil 8 ounces of water in a pot for 10 minutes.
Add the measured powdered root to the boiling water and simmer for 15 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean container.
Allow the decoction to cool before drinking it slowly throughout the day.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Elder decoctions helps with fever because they contain powerful antiviral and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive compounds in elder berries, such as anthocyanins and flavonoids, have been shown to inhibit the replication of viral pathogens responsible for causing fever. Additionally, the decoction's high water content helps to cool the body, reducing temperature and alleviating discomfort. As a natural remedy, elder decoctions offer a safe and effective way to soothe and alleviate fever symptoms without relying on harsh chemicals or pharmaceuticals.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Elderflower's anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce swelling, pain, and discomfort associated with fever.
Antiviral properties: Elderberry's antiviral compounds inhibit the replication of viruses, which can cause fever, and help to shorten the duration and severity of viral infections.
Expectorant properties: Elder's expectorant compounds help loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, making it easier to breathe and reducing congestion caused by fever.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antiviral and anti-inflammatory compounds, elder roots help reduce fever and alleviate its symptoms.
Barks: The bark contains salicin, a natural pain reliever similar to aspirin, which helps bring down high temperatures.
remedy_recipe: Gather one teaspoon of dried sambucus nigra flowers or 2-3 teaspoons of fresh flowers.
Combine the gathered herbs with one cup of boiling water in a heat-resistant glass container.
Steep the mixture for 10-15 minutes, allowing the active compounds to infuse into the water.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a separate container discarding solids.
Allow the decoction to cool before serving or storing in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow decoctions helps with fever because of its soothing and anti-inflammatory properties. The mucilage extracted from marshmallow roots coats the throat, bronchi, and lungs, providing a protective barrier against heat and irritation caused by a fever. This coating also helps to reduce inflammation and discomfort associated with respiratory issues that often accompany fever. By providing relief from these symptoms, marshmallow decoctions can help alleviate the discomfort and distress associated with fever, promoting a more comfortable recovery.
remedy_properties: Saponins: Marshmallow saponins have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help reduce inflammation and combat infection-causing bacteria and viruses associated with fever.

Mucilages: The mucilage in marshmallow roots forms a protective barrier on the mucous membranes, soothing irritation and reducing congestion caused by fever, while also providing hydration to the throat and respiratory tract.

Flavonoids: Marshmallow flavonoids exhibit antioxidant properties, which can help neutralize free radicals generated during infection, reducing oxidative stress and promoting the body's natural defense against fever.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the marshmallow plant are rich in mucilage, which helps to soothe and calm irritated respiratory tissues, making it effective in relieving fever-related congestion.
Rhizomes: The rhizomes of the marshmallow plant contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce swelling and ease pain associated with fever.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2-3 tablespoons of dried marshmallow root from a reputable supplier.
Measure 1 liter of pure filtered water and pour it over the dried herb.
Bring the mixture to a boil then reduce heat and simmer for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean container.
Store the cooled decoction in an airtight container and use within 24 hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Slippery elm decoctions helps with fever because its mucilaginous properties create a soothing and protective barrier in the respiratory tract, reducing inflammation and congestion. The decoction's cooling sensation helps to bring down body temperature by promoting sweating, while also calming the nerves and reducing restlessness. Additionally, slippery elm's anti-inflammatory compounds may help alleviate the discomfort and fatigue associated with fever, allowing for a more comfortable and quicker recovery.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Slippery elm decoction has anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce swelling and ease pain associated with fevers, making it easier for the body to recover.

Mucilaginous property: The mucilage in slippery elm decoction forms a protective film on the mucous membranes, soothing dryness and irritation caused by fever-related congestion, and providing relief from coughing and sore throats.

Antioxidant property: Slippery elm decoction contains antioxidants that help neutralize free radicals produced during the inflammatory response to fever, which can damage tissues and organs if left unchecked.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of slippery elm contain mucilage, which helps to soothe and calm the body's temperature-regulating mechanisms, making them effective in reducing fever.
Barks: Slippery elm barks have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation caused by fever, making it a valuable part for medicinal decoctions.
remedy_recipe: Gather one tablespoon of dried ulmus rubra bark per eight ounces of water for optimal dosage.
Measure two cups of cold filtered water into a saucepan and bring it to a gentle boil.
Add the measured ulmus rubra bark to the boiling water and reduce heat to low simmer.
Allow the decoction to steep for 15 minutes and then strain the liquid through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth.
Store the prepared decoction in an amber glass bottle at room temperature for up to three days before consumption.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold decoctions helps with fever because of its natural anti-inflammatory and antipyretic properties. The flavonoids and terpenes present in pot marigold have been shown to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with fever, such as headaches and body aches. Additionally, the decoction's cooling effect on the skin can help bring down high temperatures, making it an effective natural remedy for relieving feverish sensations without disrupting normal body function.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties: Calendula's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as triterpenoids and flavonoids, help reduce fever-induced inflammation and alleviate associated pain.
Antipyretic properties: The decoction's high concentration of phenolic acids, especially caffeic acid, helps lower body temperature by increasing sweating and reducing fever.

Antioxidant and immune-modulating effects: Calendula contains antioxidants like lutein and zeaxanthin, which neutralize free radicals and boost the immune system, helping to combat infections that can cause fever.

Seeds: Containing essential fatty acids and phenolic compounds, they have antipyretic properties and help soothe the body during high temperatures.
remedy_recipe: Gather high-quality dried calendula officinalis flowers weighing about 10-15 grams for every 250 milliliters of water.
Combine the dried calendula flowers with boiling water in a large glass container and allow it to steep for 20-30 minutes.
Strain the mixture using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the solid herbal particles and discard them.
Transfer the liquid decoction to a clean glass bottle and store it in the refrigerator at a temperature of around 4 degrees celsius.
Administer the calendula officinalis decoction to the patient orally every 2-3 hours, or as directed by a healthcare professional.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric decoctions helps with fever because of its unique compound curcumin, which has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. When taken as a decoction, curcumin is absorbed into the bloodstream, where it can target the source of the fever - whether it's a viral or bacterial infection - and help reduce inflammation. By reducing inflammation, turmeric decoctions can help bring down body temperature and alleviate symptoms associated with fever, such as headache, fatigue, and body aches.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Turmeric's curcuminoids reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms such as pain, swelling, and joint stiffness associated with fever.
Antimicrobial properties: The antibacterial, antiviral, and antifungal compounds in turmeric decoctions help combat underlying infections that cause fever by inhibiting the growth of pathogens.
Heat-relieving properties: Turmeric's warming and soothing effects can help to reduce body heat and alleviate symptoms such as sweating, chills, and shivering commonly experienced during a fever.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The thick, underground stems of the turmeric plant contain a high concentration of curcumin, a powerful anti-inflammatory compound that helps to reduce fever and alleviate symptoms.
Roots: Turmeric roots have been traditionally used to treat fever due to their natural antipyretic properties, which help to lower body temperature and provide relief from discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried turmeric powder equivalent to about 2-3 grams.
Bring one cup of water to a boil in a medium-sized saucepan over high heat instantly.
Add the measured turmeric powder and stir well for at least five minutes patiently.
Reduce heat to low and simmer for another ten minutes quietly allowing flavors to meld.
Strain the decoction into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth, discarding solids completely.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree decoctions helps with fever because of its unique composition of flavonoids, saponins, and terpenes. These bioactive compounds work synergistically to reduce inflammation and alleviate discomfort associated with high temperatures. The decoction's cooling properties help to lower the body's internal temperature, providing relief from sweating, headache, and fatigue. Additionally, the antioxidant-rich extracts may also boost the immune system, allowing the body to recover more efficiently from the infection or illness causing the fever.
remedy_properties: Antipyretic properties: Decoctions from Adiantum spp. contain compounds that have been shown to reduce fever by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and lowering body temperature.
Antiviral and antibacterial properties: The decoction's antimicrobial properties may help combat underlying infections that can cause fever, such as viruses or bacteria.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Adiantum spp. decoctions contain flavonoids and polyphenols that have anti-inflammatory effects, which may help alleviate symptoms associated with fever, such as headache, body aches, and fatigue.

Barks: The barks of the Maidenhair Tree have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help relieve discomfort and pain caused by fever.
remedy_recipe: Gather dried ginkgo biloba leaves measuring one teaspoon per cup of water.
Combine the dried ginkgo biloba leaves with boiling water in a heat-resistant cup.
Steep the mixture for 15-20 minutes to allow the active compounds to dissolve.
Strain the decoction through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into another container.
Store the prepared decoction in the refrigerator and use within 24 hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion decoctions helps with fever because of its potent antipyretic properties. The plant's roots, leaves, and flowers contain bioactive compounds like taraxasterol and sesquiterpenes, which work together to reduce body temperature. These compounds help to dilate blood vessels, increasing blood flow and promoting the removal of heat from the body. Additionally, dandelion decoctions have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe a sore throat and alleviate headache symptoms often associated with fever. remedy_properties: Diuretic properties: Dandelion decoction can help to increase urine production, which helps to reduce body temperature by removing excess heat and toxins from the body.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Dandelion contains anti-inflammatory compounds like taraxasterol, which can help to reduce swelling and discomfort caused by fever, making it a soothing remedy for headaches and body aches.

Antioxidant properties: Dandelion decoction is rich in antioxidants that can help to combat the oxidative stress caused by fever, reducing damage to tissues and organs, and supporting the overall recovery process.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in triterpenoid saponins, which have anti-inflammatory and antipyretic properties, making them effective against fever.
Rhyzomes: Contain sesquiterpene lactones that help reduce body temperature and alleviate symptoms of fever.
remedy_recipe: Measure out 2 teaspoons of dried taraxacum officinale root per 8 ounces of water.
Boil the mixture in a pot for 10-15 minutes to release the active compounds.
Simmer the concoction for an additional 20-25 minutes to enhance bioavailability.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean container.
Store the decoction in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours before consumption.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile decoctions helps with fever because of its natural anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which help to soothe a sore throat and calm a restless body. The apigenin in chamomile also binds to receptors in the brain, promoting relaxation and reducing stress, which can exacerbate fevers. Additionally, chamomile's antibacterial properties help to combat infections that cause fever, while its anti-inflammatory properties reduce inflammation and alleviate discomfort associated with fever. Overall, chamomile decoctions provide a natural and effective way to ease fever symptoms and promote overall wellness.
remedy_properties: Antipyretic activity: Chamomile contains apigenin, an flavonoid that has been shown to reduce body temperature and alleviate symptoms of fever by suppressing the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The azulene compounds present in chamomile have anti-inflammatory effects, which help to reduce swelling and pain associated with fever, making it a natural remedy for soothing sore throats and reducing earache discomfort.

Sedative and relaxing effects: Chamomile's terpenoids, particularly bisabolol, have been found to promote relaxation and reduce anxiety, helping to calm the mind and body during times of illness, which can aid in promoting restful sleep and reducing fever-related discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: Chamomile roots have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing fever and soothing aching joints.
Flowers: The delicate petals of chamomile flowers contain apigenin, an antioxidant that helps to cool the body and ease feverish symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Gather dried chamomile flowers measuring one teaspoon per cup of boiling water.
Boil clean filtered water in a saucepan and add the chamomile flowers.
Steep the mixture for ten to fifteen minutes allowing the flavors to meld.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a cup.
Allow the tea to cool slightly before consuming and enjoy.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Red clover decoctions helps with fever because it contains a unique combination of isoflavones, flavonoids, and saponins that have anti-inflammatory and antipyretic properties. The isoflavones in red clover, particularly biochanin A and formononetin, have been shown to reduce the body's core temperature by inhibiting prostaglandin synthesis and increasing sweating. Anti-inflammatory properties: The decoction's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as rosmarinic acid and quercetin, help reduce swelling, pain, and discomfort associated with fevers, making it easier for the body to recover from infection.

remedy_recipe: Gather one tablespoon of dried red clover flowers for each cup of water needed.
Combine the dried herb with boiling water and let it steep for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean container.
Discard the solids and store the decoction in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 ounces of fresh petroselinum crispum leaves and rinse them thoroughly under cold running water.
Combine the rinsed leaves with 8 cups of pure water in a large saucepan and bring to a boil.
Reduce heat to low and simmer for 15 minutes, allowing the mixture to infuse its medicinal properties.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass container, discarding solids.

Barks: The bark of the sage plant contains anti-inflammatory compounds that help to reduce inflammation and alleviate fever symptoms.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Feverfew decoctions helps with fever because it contains parthenolide, a chemical compound that has anti-inflammatory and antipyretic properties. remedy_recipe: Weigh 10-15 grams of dried tanacetum parthenium leaves and flowers.
Combine the herbs with 250 milliliters of boiling water in a heat-resistant container.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes to allow the active compounds to infuse into the liquid.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into another container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Catnip decoctions helps with fever because of its natural anti-inflammatory and antipyretic properties. The active compounds in catnip, such as nepetalactone and geraniol, help to reduce body temperature by increasing blood flow and promoting sweating. Additionally, catnip's antibacterial and antiviral properties can help to combat the underlying infection or illness causing the fever. This natural remedy can provide quick relief from discomfort and promote a swift recovery when used in conjunction with other forms of treatment.
remedy_properties: Antipyretic properties: Catnip contains a compound called nepetalactone, which has been shown to reduce fever by dilating blood vessels and increasing sweating, helping to lower body temperature.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Catnip's anti-inflammatory compounds can help reduce swelling and pain associated with fevers, making it easier for the body to recover.
Antimicrobial properties: Catnip's antimicrobial properties can help combat secondary bacterial infections that can occur during a fever, preventing further complications and promoting healing.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the catnip plant have a calming effect on the body, making them effective in reducing fever and alleviating symptoms associated with it.
Barks: The barks of the catnip plant contain compounds that help to reduce inflammation and ease discomfort caused by fever.
remedy_recipe: Gather high-quality dried nepeta cataria leaves and stems in the amount of one tablespoon.
Heat one cup of water in a saucepan over medium heat for five minutes.
Add the dried nepeta cataria leaves and stems to the boiling water and stir well.
Reduce heat to low and simmer the mixture for 15 to 20 minutes or until the liquid is reduced by half.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth into a clean glass container and discard the solids.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat fever with herbal decoctions is to choose herbs that have natural cooling properties. Decoctions of peppermint, eucalyptus, and yarrow are effective in reducing body temperature. Combine 1 teaspoon of dried herb with 1 cup of boiling water and steep for 10-15 minutes. Strain the mixture and drink up to three times a day. For children, use a decoction of ginger or chamomile to help soothe their fever and promote relaxation.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to fever that are treated with herbal decoctions are rheumatic pain, arthritic inflammation, and skin infections. Herbs such as turmeric, ginger, and willow bark are used in decoctions to reduce joint pain and inflammation, while herbs like neem, tea tree, and calendula help soothe skin irritations and infections. These decoctions can provide effective relief without the side effects of pharmaceutical medications.
